methods.description: Process for collecting sedimentary sequences using rotary drilling corer to study marine deposits and transgression limits.

steps.description: Consulted previous studies to identify locations where marine deposits are dominant and where the landward limit of the last marine transgression should occur.
steps.searchable_summary: review literature to identify sampling locations

steps.description: Collected 15 cores along W-E transects from the modern shoreline to 80 km inland using a rotary drilling corer.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using rotary drilling

steps.description: Levelled the surface elevations of the drilled cores to the National Yellow Sea 85 datum using a GPS-RTK system with a precision of 3 cm.
Corrected and processed GPS-RTK raw data to the National Yellow Sea 85 datum system using the CORS system network.
steps.searchable_summary: process GPS-RTK data with CORS system

methods.description: Analysis of foraminifera in sediment cores to understand historical marine environments and transgression patterns.

steps.description: Identified foraminifera in all cores except CZ01, CZ02, and DC01.
Analyzed abundance and species change of foraminifera at various depths to infer environmental changes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze foraminifera abundance and species change

methods.description: Improvement of SLIP data quality by using basal salt-marsh peat and correcting for compaction to achieve more accurate and precise data.

steps.description: Established SLIPs exclusively from basal salt-marsh peat in transgressive contact.
steps.searchable_summary: establish SLIPs from basal salt-marsh peat

steps.description: Corrected SLIP data for compaction to improve accuracy and precision.
